channel.title: NYT > World > Middle East
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: Here’s the latest.
item.dc:creator: Ephrat Livni, David E. Sanger and Yan Zhuang

item.title: Offensive vs. Defensive: On Iran, the Distinction Matters for Britain’s Leader
item.description: As the United States expands its armada of warplanes on British soil, Prime Minister Keir Starmer says he is trying to defend Britain’s interests while keeping the country out of war.
item.dc:creator: Adam Goldman, Eric Schmitt and Michael D. Shear
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Prime Minister Keir Starmer of Britain at a meeting in London on Monday. “We will not be drawn into the wider war,” he told lawmakers in mid-March.

item.title: Kuwaiti Oil Tanker Set Ablaze After Attack Off the Coast of Dubai
item.description: The attack damaged the hull, the owner of the tanker said, which could cause an oil spill.
item.dc:creator: Ephrat Livni
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: The Dubai port in the United Arab Emirates in 2022.

item.title: Here’s What Happened in the War in the Middle East on Monday
item.description: Iran maintained that no negotiations have been held with the United States, and said none would while the fighting continues.
item.dc:creator: Ephrat Livni
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
item.media:description: Residents viewing the damage to their apartment in Tehran after airstrikes on Monday.

item.title: Iran advances a plan to charge a toll for ships to traverse the Strait of Hormuz.
item.dc:creator: Ephrat Livni
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: The Callisto tanker anchored in Muscat, Oman, this month.

item.title: A New U.S. Missile Hit a Sports Hall in Iran. Here’s What We Know About It.
item.description: Barely out of prototype testing, the Precision Strike Missile is shrouded in secrecy — including which Persian Gulf countries the Army is launching them from.
item.dc:creator: John Ismay
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces

item.title: Iran’s Fractured Leadership Is Struggling to Coordinate, Officials Say
item.description: As leaders are killed and replaced, Iranian negotiators may not know what their government is willing to concede in any negotiations.
item.dc:creator: Julian E. Barnes, Adam Goldman and Ronen Bergman
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
item.media:description: Many senior Iranian leaders and their deputies have been killed, and those who survive have had difficulty communicating.

item.title: Trump Zigzags on Iran, Claiming ‘Great Progress’ but Making Threats
item.description: President Trump threatened to hit vital infrastructure in Iran if the Strait of Hormuz was not reopened, but he also said that “regime change” had already been achieved.
item.dc:creator: Aaron Boxerman, Erika Solomon, Sanam Mahoozi and Thomas Fuller
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Eric Lee for The New York Times
item.media:description: President Trump arriving at the White House Sunday night after returning from Florida.

item.title: Iran Condemns Attacks on Its Universities, Warns of Retaliation
item.description: After attacks on several Iranian campuses, Iranian forces warn they could target American universities in the region.
item.dc:creator: Erika Solomon and Sanam Mahoozi
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

item.title: Analysis shows where a new U.S. missile hit an Iranian sports hall and school.
item.dc:creator: Leanne Abraham and Christiaan Triebert

item.title: Lawsuit Accuses Americans of Political Killings for Hire Overseas
item.description: Three men working for an American security contractor say the United Arab Emirates paid them to carry out targeted killings in Yemen. One of their targets is suing them in U.S. federal court.
item.dc:creator: Vivian Nereim
item.media:credit: Giles Clarke for The New York Times
item.media:description: Aden, Yemen, in 2018. It became the capital in exile for the internationally recognized Yemeni government, and was badly damaged by airstrikes in 2015.

item.title: Two More U.N. Peacekeepers Are Killed in Southern Lebanon
item.description: The deaths came a day after the U.N. secretary-general, António Guterres, condemned the killing of another peacekeeper.
item.dc:creator: Euan Ward
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: U.N. peacekeepers in southern Lebanon last week.

item.title: Trump Threatens to Destroy Kharg Island and Other Key Iranian Energy Sites
item.description: President Trump said in a newspaper interview that the United States might try to invade the Persian Gulf island, which is Iran’s main oil export hub.
item.dc:creator: Amelia Nierenberg
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Fatemeh Bahrami/Anadolu Agency, via Getty Images
item.media:description: The Port of Kharg Island Oil Terminal in Iran in 2017.

item.title: Israel Pulls Battalion From West Bank After Soldier Talks of ‘Revenge’
item.description: While detaining a CNN crew, a soldier said that Israeli troops were motivated by “revenge” against Palestinians and that all of the West Bank was “for the Jews.”
item.dc:creator: David M. Halbfinger
item.media:description: Israeli soldiers in the occupied West Bank earlier this month.

item.title: Trump Claims ‘Regime Change’ in Iran Is Already Complete
item.description: President Trump suggested that Iran’s current leaders were “much more reasonable” as he sought to show progress in his war aims. Iran’s power structure appears firmly in control despite a month of U.S.-Israeli attacks.
item.dc:creator: Amelia Nierenberg
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
Coups D'Etat and Attempted Coups D'Etat
item.media:credit: Tierney L. Cross/The New York Times
item.media:description: President Trump speaking aboard Air Force One, in Florida, on Sunday.

item.title: Israel Passes Law to Hang Palestinians Convicted of Deadly Attacks
item.description: Experts say the law was written in such a way as to ensure that it was unlikely to ever apply to Jewish extremists who commit similar crimes.
item.dc:creator: Aaron Boxerman and Johnatan Reiss
item.media:description: Itamar Ben-Gvir, center, the national security minister, and other Israeli hard-liners had campaigned on a promise to execute Palestinian militants.

item.title: Can the ‘Dubai Dream’ Survive the War? Residents Say Life Goes On.
item.description: The Emirates’ largest city pitched itself to foreign workers and tourists as a sun-soaked safe haven in a volatile region. War has challenged that image.
item.dc:creator: Raja Abdulrahim and Katarina Premfors
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Katarina Premfors for The New York Times

item.title: Here’s the latest.
item.dc:creator: Ephrat Livni, David E. Sanger, Aaron Boxerman and Erika Solomon

item.title: New U.S. Missile Hit Iranian Sports Hall and School, Analysis Shows
item.description: The Pentagon used missiles untested in combat in a deadly attack that struck civilian sites near a military compound on Feb. 28, according to visual evidence examined by The Times and weapons experts.
item.dc:creator: Christiaan Triebert and John Ismay
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces
US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

item.title: Trump Says Iran Agreed to Allow 20 More Ships of Oil Through Strait of Hormuz
item.description: The president, speaking to reporters on Air Force One, cast the permission as a “sign of respect.” He also asserted that the United States had already achieved “regime change” in Iran.
item.dc:creator: David E. Sanger
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Ships anchored in Muscat, Oman, near the Strait of Hormuz, on Wednesday.

item.title: Here’s What Happened in the War in the Middle East on Sunday
item.description: Diplomats from around the region met in Pakistan, and the U.S.-Israeli bombardment hit a southern Iranian port, killing at least five people, Iranian state media said.
item.dc:creator: Anushka Patil
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
item.media:description: Airstrikes damaged the Tehran headquarters of Al Araby TV on Sunday.

item.title: Catholic Leaders Barred From Jerusalem Holy Site on Palm Sunday
item.description: Israeli police stopped clergymen from celebrating the Palm Sunday Mass at the Church of the Holy Sepulcher “for the first time in centuries,” church officials said.
item.dc:creator: Johnatan Reiss
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Cardinal Pierbattista Pizzaballa leading a prayer service to mark Palm Sunday in Jerusalem, amid restrictions on gathering in large groups because of the war in Iran.

item.title: Iran War Draws Attention to U.S. Troop Presence in Saudi Arabia
item.description: A close U.S. ally and regional rival of Iran, the kingdom has faced hundreds of Iranian missile and drone attacks since the war began.
item.dc:creator: Vivian Nereim
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Eric Lee for The New York Times
item.media:description: The dignified transfer at Dover Air Force Base in Dover, Del., of Sgt. Benjamin N. Pennington, who was killed at Prince Sultan Air Base in Saudi Arabia earlier in March.

item.title: Israel’s military posted two images of Ali Choeib in what it said was a Hezbollah uniform. One was fake.
item.description: The
item.dc:creator: Isabel Kershner

item.title: Regional officials meet in Pakistan to discuss the war in Iran.
item.description: Pakistani officials hosted talks on Sunday with the foreign ministers of Saudi Arabia, Turkey and Egypt, in the country’s latest efforts to mediate the war in the Middle East.
item.dc:creator: Zia ur-Rehman and Salman Masood
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: A motorcade arriving at the Ministry of Foreign Affairs office in Islamabad, Pakistan, on Sunday.

item.title: The U.S. Now Has Over 50,000 Troops in the Middle East
item.description: The arrival of 2,500 Marines and another 2,500 sailors is keeping the number of American troops in the region at roughly 10,000 more than usual.
item.dc:creator: Helene Cooper
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:description: The U.S.S. Tripoli entering the Singapore Strait earlier this month. The 2,500 Marines who have been dispatched to the Middle East are part of the U.S.S. Tripoli amphibious ready group.

item.title: Hundreds Gather for Funeral of 3 Lebanese Killed in Israeli Strike
item.description: Israel said one of those killed was a member of Hezbollah’s military wing “disguised as a journalist.” Lebanon’s president said all three were journalists and condemned the killings.
item.dc:creator: Abdi Latif Dahir and Hwaida Saad
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

item.title: Why the Iran War May Force Countries to Rely Less on Natural Gas
item.description: The U.S. and other exporters are poised for a windfall, but disruptions to Persian Gulf supplies are also pushing gas-buying countries to consider alternatives like coal, solar and nuclear energy.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca F. Elliott and Brad Plumer
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
Far East, South and Southeast Asia and Pacific Areas
item.media:description: A liquefied natural gas plant in Cameron, La., last year. The United States, the world’s biggest gas exporter, will almost certainly benefit from shortages caused by the war in Iran.

item.title: Here’s the latest.
item.dc:creator: David E. Sanger, Aaron Boxerman and Sanam Mahoozi

item.title: A Toothless Iran? Missile and Drone Strikes Show It Can Still Inflict Pain.
item.description: A wave of strikes across the Middle East in recent days shows that Iran has not lost the capacity to retaliate.
item.dc:creator: Nicholas Kulish, Helene Cooper, Isabel Kershner and Erika Solomon
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Amit Elkayam for The New York Times
item.media:description: Emergency responders at the site of a strike in the town of Eshtaol, Israel, on Saturday.

item.title: Here’s What Happened in the War in the Middle East on Saturday
item.description: The war with Iran escalated on Saturday as the Iran-allied Houthis joined the conflict and Israel bombarded Tehran with airstrikes.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Ahn
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Amit Elkayam for The New York Times
item.media:description: Emergency responders at the site of a strike in Eshtaol, Israel, on Saturday.

item.title: Airstrikes batter a university and residential zones in Tehran.
item.dc:creator: Pranav Baskar and Shirin Hakim
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
item.media:description: A Red Crescent Society worker at an auto service center targeted by U.S.-Israeli airstrikes in Tehran on Saturday.

item.title: In Dubai, a famed horse race goes on, despite the war.
item.dc:creator: Raja Abdulrahim
item.media:description: The start of a race at Meydan Racecourse in Dubai on Saturday.

item.title: Ukraine Finalizes Air Defense Deals With Gulf Nations Amid War in Mideast
item.description: The Ukrainian president visited the Middle East, where he sought to shift his country from a recipient of military aid to a supplier.
item.dc:creator: Andrew E. Kramer
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Ukrainian Presidential Press Service, via Agence France-Presse
item.media:description: In a photo released by the Ukrainian Presidential Press Service, President Volodymyr Zelensky, in black, and Emir Sheikh Tamim bin Hamad al-Thani of Qatar, center left, walk together during their meeting in Doha, Qatar, on Saturday.

item.title: Israeli Strike Kills 3 Journalists in Southern Lebanon, Officials Say
item.description: Israel accused one of the reporters of being a Hezbollah operative. Lebanon’s president said they were journalists and condemned the killings.
item.dc:creator: Euan Ward
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
War Crimes, Genocide and Crimes Against Humanity
item.media:description: A police officer checks a charred car in the town of Jezzine in southern Lebanon on Saturday, after an Israeli strike that killed three Lebanese journalists.

item.title: Pakistan Set to Host Top Regional Diplomats for Talks on Iran War
item.description: The foreign ministers of Saudi Arabia, Turkey and Egypt are expected in Islamabad on Sunday as the Pakistani government strives to find a diplomatic solution to the war.
item.dc:creator: Zia ur-Rehman
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Dave Sanders for The New York Times
item.media:description: Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif of Pakistan at the United Nations in 2024. His country has recently intensified efforts at shuttle diplomacy between the United States and Iran.

item.title: Yemen’s Houthis Fire at Israel and Vow Further Attacks
item.description: There has long been concern that the Houthis, an Iran-backed militia, could disrupt shipping in the Red Sea if they entered the broader war.
item.dc:creator: Abdi Latif Dahir and Eve Sampson
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Houthi supporters in Sanaa, Yemen, demonstrating in solidarity with Iran on Friday.

item.title: Here’s the latest.
item.dc:creator: Aaron Boxerman, Eric Schmitt, Chris Cameron and John Yoon

item.title: Iranian Strike on U.S. Base in Saudi Arabia Injures 12 American Troops, Officials Say
item.description: The combined missile and drone attack amounted to one of the most serious breaches of American air defenses in the course of the monthlong war with Iran.
item.dc:creator: Chris Cameron and Eric Schmitt
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:description: Satellite image shows planes at Prince Sultan Air Base in Saudi Arabia, last month.

item.title: Here’s What Happened in the War in the Middle East on Friday
item.description: American officials sought to temper concerns of a possibly expanding military incursion after the Pentagon’s decision to deploy more troops to the Middle East.
item.dc:creator: Eve Sampson
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Diego Ibarra Sanchez for The New York Times
item.media:description: Israel on Friday launched airstrikes on Dahiya, an area on the outskirts of Lebanon’s capital that is a Hezbollah stronghold.

item.title: Strikes on Iranian Industry Expand Blows to Civilian Economy
item.description: After Israel said it would intensify attacks on Iran’s industrial infrastructure, major steel plants and other vital plants were struck.
item.dc:creator: Farnaz Fassihi and Shirin Hakim
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Mobarakeh Steel Complex in Isfahan, Iran, in 2012. The governor of Isfahan said one person was killed and 15 were injured when Israel attacked the complex on Friday.

item.title: Iran Moves to Assert Control Over Strait While Trading Strikes With Israel
item.description: Two ships turned back after being warned not to pass through the Strait of Hormuz on Friday. Iran said strikes in the country had hit a uranium processing plant and industrial sites.
item.dc:creator: Euan Ward, Yeganeh Torbati, Adam Rasgon and Michael Levenson
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
item.media:description: Cleaning up rubble in an apartment damaged by airstrikes in Tehran on Friday.

item.title: Government Trolls Sling Memes in the Online Trenches of Mideast War
item.description: Officials in Tehran and Washington alike are trading taunts in English, often using American pop culture references.
item.dc:creator: Ephrat Livni and Parin Behrooz
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
item.media:description: A billboard in Tehran this month showing Iran’s three supreme leaders since the Islamic Revolution.

item.title: Europe Is Drafting Postwar Plan to Escort Tankers, Officials Say
item.description: France and Britain are leading planning to have warships escort vessels through the Strait of Hormuz, which officials say is farther along than has been revealed publicly.
item.dc:creator: Catherine Porter and Lara Jakes
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Tankers anchored in Muscat, Oman, earlier this month, as the Strait of Hormuz remained largely closed to shipping traffic.

item.title: Elon Musk Joins in Phone Call Between Donald Trump and Narendra Modi
item.description: Mr. Musk’s participation was notable because it is rare for a private citizen to be on a call between heads of state. It also suggests that Mr. Musk is back on better terms with the president.
item.dc:creator: Edward Wong, Theodore Schleifer, Tyler Pager and Ryan Mac
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Eric Lee for The New York Times
item.media:description: Elon Musk and Shivon Zilis arriving for a wedding at Mar-a-Lago last month.

item.title: Iran Tried to Use a Famed Director’s Home to Push Its War Narrative. His Son Fired Back.
item.description: The Tehran home of the acclaimed Iranian director Abbas Kiarostami was damaged in strikes this week. His son Ahmad pushed back on efforts by the government to seize on his legacy.
item.dc:creator: Yeganeh Torbati
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: The filmmaker Abbas Kiarostami at a Film Festival in Dubai in 2012.

item.title: U.N. Report Documents Sectarian Violence in Syria That Killed Over 1,700
item.description: Investigators said the government of President Ahmed al-Sharaa had done little to address the extrajudicial killings and torture of Druse civilians in July 2025.
item.dc:creator: Nick Cumming-Bruce
item.category: War Crimes, Genocide and Crimes Against Humanity
item.media:credit: Nanna Heitmann for The New York Times
item.media:description: Bodies recovered from Syrian villages were taken to Sweida National Hospital in Syria for identification last year.

item.title: Iran Moves to Formalize Toll Plan in Strait of Hormuz
item.description: Tehran has effectively closed off the critical waterway, turning back container ships on Friday, and Iranian lawmakers are considering whether to formalize charging fees to pass.
item.dc:creator: Euan Ward
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Iran has effectively cut off traffic through the Strait of Hormuz, leaving thousands of vessels idling on either side of it.

item.title: Global Food Supply Faces a Dangerous Bottleneck as Iran War Persists
item.description: Fertilizer prices are climbing as a result of disruptions in the Middle East, putting global food supplies at risk.
item.dc:creator: Ana Swanson
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: A farmer spread fertilizer on his mustard field this week in Pampore, India. Large parts of Asia, particularly India and Thailand, are most exposed to fertilizer supply shortages.
